question.text: Inflation risk during the distribution stage means that:

answer.text: The nominal value of the retirement corpus will decrease over time
The purchasing power of fixed retirement income will erode over time, requiring more money to maintain the same lifestyle
The interest rates on fixed deposits will increase
The equity markets will crash during retirement

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Inflation risk means the same amount of money buys less over time. A fixed pension of Rs.60,000/month will have significantly reduced purchasing power after 10-15 years of inflation. **The Trap:** Inflation risk is not about nominal corpus reduction or market crashes — it is specifically about purchasing power erosion. **Cross-Exam Context:** This risk is why even retirees are advised to maintain 25-30% equity exposure — to generate returns that keep pace with or exceed inflation. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Structure retirement income with a mix of fixed and inflation-linked sources. Use equity SWPs or inflation-indexed bonds to provide the variable component.
